name: airtable-formula
description: Airtable formula editor, MCP server installer, and AI skills for VS Code.

commands.title: Airtable Formula: Switch MCP Tool Profile
Airtable Formula: Toggle MCP Tool Category
Airtable Formula: Show MCP Tool Status
Airtable Formula: Open MCP Tool Config File
Airtable Formula: Stop & Export Debug Log

airtableFormula.mcp.autoConfigureOnInsta.description: Automatically configure MCP in detected IDEs on first launch.

airtableFormula.mcp.serverPathOverride.description: Override bundled MCP server path. Leave empty to use bundled.

airtableFormula.mcp.notifyOnUpdates.description: Show notification when MCP server updates are available.

enumDescriptions: Use the MCP server bundled with the extension (default)
Use npx to run airtable-user-mcp from npm (version-independent)
airtableFormula.mcp.serverSource.description: How the MCP server is launched for external IDEs.

airtableFormula.ai.autoInstallFiles.description: Automatically install AI skills/rules/workflows on first launch.

airtableFormula.ai.includeAgents.description: Install agent config files where the IDE supports them.

airtableFormula.formula.defaultBeautifyS.description: Default beautify style (readable, compact, smart, etc.).

airtableFormula.auth.autoRefresh.description: Automatically check and refresh Airtable session on a schedule.

airtableFormula.auth.refreshIntervalHour.description: Hours between automatic session health checks (1–168).

airtableFormula.auth.loginMode.description: Login mode: 'manual' opens a browser for you to log in, 'auto' uses stored credentials for automated login.

airtableFormula.auth.browserChoice.description: Browser selection for authentication. Set via the dashboard UI.

enumDescriptions: Schema inspection and formula validation only (7 tools)
Read + create/update tables, fields, and views, no deletes (26 tools)
All tools enabled including destructive and extensions (36 tools)
airtableFormula.mcp.toolProfile.description: Active MCP tool profile. Controls which tools are exposed to AI agents through the bundled Airtable MCP server.

airtableFormula.mcp.categories.read.description: Read / Inspect tools: get_base_schema, list_tables, get_table_schema, list_fields, list_views, get_view, validate_formula.

airtableFormula.mcp.categories.fieldWrit.description: Field Write tools: create_field, create_formula_field, update_field_config, update_formula_field, rename_field, update_field_description, duplicate_field.

airtableFormula.mcp.categories.viewWrite.description: View Write tools: create_view, duplicate_view, rename_view, update_view_description, update_view_filters, reorder_view_fields, show_or_hide_view_columns, apply_view_sorts, update_view_group_levels, update_view_row_height.

airtableFormula.mcp.categories.extension.description: Extension Management tools: create_extension, create_extension_dashboard, install_extension, update_extension_state, rename_extension, duplicate_extension, remove_extension.

airtableFormula.mcp.tools.description: Per-tool enable/disable overrides (only used in 'custom' profile). Keys are tool names, values are booleans.

airtableFormula.debug.enabled.description: Enable debug event collection for both extension and MCP server. Events are buffered in memory and only written to disk on export.

airtableFormula.debug.bufferSize.description: Maximum number of debug events to keep in the ring buffer.

airtableFormula.debug.verboseHttp.description: Include HTTP request/response events from the MCP server (high volume).

mcpServerDefinitionProviders.label: Airtable User MCP
mcpServerDefinitionProviders.description: Manage Airtable bases via 36 MCP tools: schema read, table CRUD, field CRUD (including formula/rollup/lookup), view configuration (filters / sorts / groups / visibility — with merge-safe append mode), formula validation, and extension management. Operates over stdio using JSON-RPC 2.0.

scripts.build: tsup src/extension.ts --format cjs --dts --external vscode --out-dir dist && node -e "require('fs').cpSync('src/vendor','dist/vendor',{recursive:true})"
scripts.package:vsix: npm run build && npm run build:mcp && npm run prepare:deps && node -e "let r=require('fs').readFileSync('../../README.md','utf8');r=r.replace(//g,'');require('fs').writeFileSync('README.md',r)" && npm run package
